title: Even and Odd Functions
summary: This page introduces even and odd functions, defining them mathematically by their symmetry properties about the vertical axis. It presents the condition xe(t) = xe(-t) for even functions and xo(t) = -xo(-t) for odd functions, and lists properties governing products of even and odd functions. Graphical examples of each function type are also provided.

figures.caption: Even and odd components of a signal, showing (a) the even part x_e(t) symmetric about the vertical axis and (b) the odd part x_o(t) antisymmetric about the vertical axis.
figures.description: The figure has two time-domain plots with horizontal axis labeled t and a vertical axis at t = 0. In panel (a), the signal labeled x_e(t) is drawn above the axis and is symmetric about the vertical axis, with matching values indicated at t = -a and t = a by dashed guide lines. In panel (b), the signal labeled x_o(t) is antisymmetric about the vertical axis, showing equal magnitude and opposite sign at mirrored times such as -a and a. Students should notice that even components satisfy mirror symmetry, while odd components change sign under reflection about t = 0.
keywords: mirror points minus a and a
